classification.cautionReason: Conciliar or creedal material received in mainstream Nicene Christian tradition, with denominational differences on some canons.

metadata.author: The Canons of the Council in Trullo; Often Called The Quinisext Council

title: Canon XXXIV.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
verses.text: But in future, since the priestly canon openly sets this forth, that the crime of conspiracy or secret society is forbidden by external laws, but much more ought it to be prohibited in the Church; we also hasten to observe that if any clerics or monks are found either conspiring or entering secret societies, or devising anything against bishops or clergymen, they shall be altogether deprived of their rank.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
verses.text: If clerics or monks enter into conspiracies or fraternities, or plots against the bishop or their fellow clerics, they shall be cast out of their grade.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
verses.text: This is but a renewal of Canon xviij. of Chalcedon, which see with the notes.
